name: mcp-server-rt
description: MCP server for RT (Request Tracker) — search tickets, manage queues, add comments and replies, and update ticket fields.
long_description: Connects Claude to a live RT (Request Tracker) instance via the REST2 API. Supports searching tickets with TicketSQL, reading ticket details and history, creating and updating tickets (including custom fields, custom roles, links, and date fields), adding comments and replies, and looking up queues and users.

tools.name: search_tickets
tools.description: Search for tickets using RT's TicketSQL query language

tools.name: get_ticket
tools.description: Get detailed information about a specific ticket by its ID

tools.name: get_ticket_history
tools.description: Get the transaction history for a ticket

tools.name: get_transaction
tools.description: Get the full details of a single transaction including decoded message content

tools.name: get_queue
tools.description: Get details about a specific queue by ID or name

tools.name: get_queue_fields
tools.description: Get custom field definitions and lifecycle name for a queue

tools.name: lookup_user
tools.description: Search for RT users by name or email address

tools.name: get_current_user
tools.description: Get the RT user account associated with the configured auth token

tools.name: get_ticketsql_grammar
tools.description: Returns the TicketSQL grammar reference for RT 6.0.2

tools.name: create_ticket
tools.description: Create a new ticket in RT

tools.name: update_ticket
tools.description: Update fields on an existing ticket

tools.name: add_comment
tools.description: Add an internal comment to a ticket (not visible to the requestor)

tools.name: add_reply
tools.description: Send a reply (correspondence) on a ticket, visible to the requestor

tools.name: get_ticket_attachments
tools.description: List all attachments on a ticket

tools.name: get_attachment
tools.description: Retrieve a single attachment by ID

tools.name: save_attachment
tools.description: Save an attachment to a local file

rt_url.title: RT URL
rt_url.description: The base URL of your RT instance (e.g. https://rt.example.com)

rt_token.title: RT Auth Token
rt_token.description: RT authentication token. Create one in RT under: Logged in as → Settings → Auth Tokens
